items.title:
Regarding the results of the G7 Summit.

First, I received a positive signal from the entire G7, including President Trump, on strengthening Ukraine’s air defense. For the first time, the idea of licensing the production of missiles is seen positively by the American side. Now, they will hold all the necessary consultations with Raytheon and other manufacturers. I really hope they come back with a positive answer.

Second, no one sees that Putin is willing to sit down and end this war through dialogue. There’s a sense that even people around him understand that he can’t occupy Ukraine and win this war. We very much want this war to end as soon as possible. For that, we need sanctions. And President Trump said he would return to this policy. That’s also a good signal.

Third, we will be receiving significantly more support. These are President Trump’s words. The goal is for Putin to understand that support will not stop, so it would be better for him to stop the war.

From the joint press interaction with the President of Honduras, Nasry Asfura. (2/3)

items.title:
I think the rounds of negotiations with the Russian side will resume. The question is in what format. Europeans are in favor of it being a joint track with the Americans. And everyone also supports the track we have proposed.

I believe in a bilateral meeting. But it’s important that partners are present. Because there are many things Ukraine is counting on. Security guarantees after the end of the war. Membership in the European Union. We are giving the Russians the option to choose the format.

From the joint press interaction with the President of Honduras, Nasry Asfura. (3/3)

items.title:
Today marks the first visit by a President of Honduras to Ukraine since we regained our independence. It is very important that Ukraine’s partners come here and see with their own eyes what Ukrainians are going through. That is why we are grateful for this visit at this particular moment.

We discussed with Nasry Asfura the key directions for the development of our bilateral relations: trade, the agricultural sector, the economy, and digitalization. Honduras is interested in this, and we are ready to help here. Security was also discussed. Today, in military technologies, particularly drones, Ukraine is among the strongest in the world. We are ready to help those who help us.

I thank the President and his team for supporting us on all key geopolitical platforms. On the one hand, Ukraine must defend itself, and we need air defenses to protect the lives of our people. On the other hand, we need the same level of protection for our geopolitical interests so that this Russian war ultimately ends with a reliable peace. Thank you!

items.title: Ukraine drone strike brings war to streets of Moscow: ‘this is the new reality’
items.summary:
In the Moscow district of Maryino on Friday, shopkeeper Andrei Kondratyev braced for more Ukrainian attacks and possible petrol shortages, saying Russians needed to “get used to” a new reality.
A day earlier, Kyiv set an oil refinery ablaze in the nearby Kapotnya area in its biggest drone attack on Moscow in years, engulfing the Russian capital in smoke.

items.summary:
